tagline: Work with Chatwoot support-desk data and approved account-scoped mutations.

commands.notes: Bounded direct read; response JSON is capped and secret-shaped keys are redacted.

commands.risk: Creates a new Chatwoot conversation in the configured account and may become customer-visible through the inbox channel.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: External source identifier for the conversation.

commands.risk: Changes a conversation's workflow status and can affect routing, reporting, and agent queues.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: The messages stream fans out across conversations; single-conversation message direct reads are planned separately.

commands.risk: Sends a message into a Chatwoot conversation; customer-visible unless private is true and may trigger notifications.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Attachment/multipart variants remain blocked until the binary policy slice.
examples: pm chatwoot message send --conversation-id 42 --content 'Follow-up sent from approved plan' --json

help_topics.name: chatwoot-auth
help_topics.summary: Use a saved Chatwoot API access token; never pass token values in command text.

help_topics.name: chatwoot-writes
help_topics.summary: Chatwoot reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute; destructive/admin operations use typed fixed commands and confirmation metadata.

help_topics.name: chatwoot-direct-reads
help_topics.summary: Direct-read commands are implemented for official Chatwoot GET operations with bounded JSON output, except duplicate/disallowed rows; mutation endpoints remain governed by reverse-ETL policy.
